name: Tourist Places API
description: REST API for discovering and managing tourist destinations: countries, categories, places, activities, reviews, and user-curated place lists—with JWT auth, Google OAuth2, and PostgreSQL on AWS RDS.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Scattered travel data with no unified API
problemStatement.problemDescription: Travel apps and content sites need a single backend to browse countries, categorize destinations, read reviews, and let users save personal place lists. Without a structured API, frontends duplicate validation, auth, and rating logic.
problemList: No shared catalog of countries, tourist places, categories, and activities
User-generated reviews and ratings stored inconsistently across clients
Account activation, password reset, and OAuth flows reimplemented per app
Hard to deploy consistently across environments without container + migration strategy
Public browse vs authenticated write operations need clear security boundaries

solution.solutionTitle: Modular Spring Boot REST API for tourist content

solutionList.title: Domain modules with clear boundaries
solutionList.description: Separate packages for auth, users, countries, places, activities, and reviews—each with controllers, services, DTOs, and repositories.

solutionList.title: JWT + Google OAuth2 authentication
solutionList.description: Email/password signup with activation tokens, refresh tokens, Redis-backed logout blacklist, and optional Google social login.

solutionList.title: Rich place discovery
solutionList.description: Search, filter by country/category, paginated listings, and user-curated place lists for trip planning.

solutionList.title: Schema-managed PostgreSQL
solutionList.description: Flyway migrations version the relational model; JPA validates against RDS in production.

solutionList.title: AWS-ready container deploy
solutionList.description: Docker image on EC2 connects to external RDS PostgreSQL and Redis/ElastiCache—no bundled database in compose.

metricsList: 6 domain modules (auth, user, country, places, activity, review)
Flyway V1 migration with 8 core tables

mediaGallery.title: Tourist Places API — product views
mediaGallery.description: Portfolio screenshots and diagrams. Replace placeholder URLs with Swagger UI captures and architecture diagrams from your AWS deployment.

metrics.label: Domain modules
metrics.description: auth, user, country, places, activity, review

metrics.label: Java runtime
metrics.description: Eclipse Temurin in Docker multi-stage Maven build

metrics.label: Auth
metrics.description: Bearer tokens with Redis blacklist; Google OAuth2 optional

layers.name: Presentation (clients)
layers.description: Web and mobile clients for browsing destinations and managing user lists.
responsibilities: Store JWT securely (memory or secure storage)

layers.name: API gateway & edge
layers.description: TLS termination and routing to Spring Boot on EC2.

layers.name: Application layer
layers.description: Spring Boot 3 monolith in Docker with modular packages under at.backend.tourist.places.
components: modules/user — profiles, place lists, user reviews

layers.name: Data & cache
layers.description: Persistent PostgreSQL on RDS; Redis for tokens; Caffeine for hot country reads.
responsibilities: ACID storage for places, users, reviews
Token blacklist and short-lived activation keys
Reduce DB load on country browse endpoints

designPatterns.title: Layered architecture
designPatterns.description: Controllers delegate to services; services use repositories and mappers—classic Spring layering.

designPatterns.title: DTO + Mapper
designPatterns.description: MapStruct and ModelMapper translate JPA entities to API DTOs, keeping persistence out of controllers.

designPatterns.title: Factory — token generators
designPatterns.description: TokenFactory selects AccessTokenGenerator, RefreshTokenGenerator, or activation/reset generators by type.

designPatterns.title: Filter chain — JWT
designPatterns.description: JwtAuthenticationFilter runs before UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter for stateless Bearer auth.

designPatterns.title: Global exception handler
designPatterns.description: CustomGlobalExceptionHandler maps validation and domain exceptions to HTTP status codes.

designPatterns.title: Cache-aside
designPatterns.description: CountryService methods annotated with @Cacheable backed by Caffeine CacheManager.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless API containers
scalabilityStrategies.description: Run multiple app containers on EC2 or scale to additional instances behind ALB; JWT avoids server sessions.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Amazon RDS PostgreSQL
scalabilityStrategies.description: Managed database with automated backups, Multi-AZ option, and storage autoscaling for growing place/review data.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Redis for shared token state
scalabilityStrategies.description: ElastiCache or Upstash lets multiple API instances share blacklist and activation token storage.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Caffeine for read-heavy country data
scalabilityStrategies.description: In-process cache reduces RDS queries on public country endpoints until cache invalidation strategy is needed.

securityStrategies.title: JWT Bearer authentication
securityStrategies.description: Access tokens signed with Base64-decoded HMAC secret; validated on each protected request.

securityStrategies.title: Redis token blacklist
securityStrategies.description: Logout and one-time tokens stored in Redis to prevent replay.

securityStrategies.title: Role model (VIEWER/EDITOR/ADMIN)
securityStrategies.description: Roles embedded in JWT claims; admin routes intended for elevated access—needs @PreAuthorize enforcement.

securityStrategies.title: CSRF disabled for REST
securityStrategies.description: Stateless API disables CSRF—acceptable for Bearer-token clients; not for cookie sessions.

securityStrategies.title: Flyway + ddl-auto validate
securityStrategies.description: Schema changes only via migrations; Hibernate validates entity mapping at startup.

cacheStrategies.name: Redis token store
cacheStrategies.description: Activation, reset, and blacklist keys via RedisTokenService

cacheStrategies.name: JPA second-level
cacheStrategies.description: Not enabled — rely on Caffeine for explicit cache names only

architectureFeatures.title: ResponseWrapper envelope
architectureFeatures.description: Consistent JSON shape with success, data, message, and status_code across controllers.

architectureFeatures.title: OpenAPI-first docs
architectureFeatures.description: springdoc generates live schema from Spring MVC controllers and custom operation annotations.

architectureFeatures.title: Modular packages
architectureFeatures.description: Each domain (country, places, review) is a self-contained module with controller/service/repository.

architectureFeatures.title: Cloud-first compose
architectureFeatures.description: docker-compose.yml comments out local Postgres; expects RDS connection string in .env.

requestFlow.title: Client request
requestFlow.description: Client calls REST endpoint; protected routes include Authorization Bearer header.

requestFlow.title: ALB & Spring Security
requestFlow.description: ALB forwards to EC2:8080; JwtAuthenticationFilter validates token and sets SecurityContext.

requestFlow.title: Controller & service
requestFlow.description: Controller validates DTO, delegates to service (e.g. TouristPlaceService.searchTouristPlaces).

requestFlow.title: Persistence
requestFlow.description: JPA repository reads/writes RDS; country reads may hit Caffeine cache.

requestFlow.title: ResponseWrapper
requestFlow.description: JSON response with success flag, data payload, and HTTP-aligned status_code.

eventFlow.title: User signup
eventFlow.description: AuthService generates activation token and queues email via SendingService.

eventFlow.title: Token in Redis
eventFlow.description: Short-lived activation/reset token stored in Redis for validation.

eventFlow.title: Account activation
eventFlow.description: User POSTs token to /auth/activate-account/{token}; UserService marks account activated.

decisions.title: Spring Boot monolith vs microservices
decisions.problem: Solo/small-team tourism API needs fast iteration and simple AWS ops.
decisions.solution: Single Spring Boot JAR in Docker on EC2 with package-per-domain modules.
decisions.outcome: One deploy artifact; scale EC2/RDS first before splitting services.

decisions.title: PostgreSQL on RDS
decisions.problem: Relational model with FKs between countries, places, reviews, and users.
decisions.solution: Flyway-managed schema on Amazon RDS PostgreSQL; H2 only in tests.
decisions.outcome: Strong integrity for ratings and place relationships; RDS handles backups.

decisions.title: JJWT + Redis blacklist
decisions.problem: SPA clients need stateless auth with logout support.
decisions.solution: Custom JwtService with Redis-backed blacklist instead of session cookies.
decisions.outcome: Works behind ALB; requires Redis availability in AWS.

decisions.title: Caffeine for countries
decisions.problem: Country list endpoints are read-heavy and change infrequently.
decisions.solution: In-process Caffeine cache with named caches per query pattern.
decisions.outcome: Simple and fast for single-instance EC2; consider Redis cache when horizontally scaling.

codeExamples.title: JWT validation with Redis blacklist
codeExamples.description: Every authenticated request passes through JwtService which rejects blacklisted tokens before parsing claims.

files.name: JwtService.java
files.explanation: Blacklist check runs before JJWT parse; supports 6-char activation tokens via RedisTokenService.

codeExamples.title: JWT authentication filter
codeExamples.description: OncePerRequestFilter extracts Bearer token, validates it, and populates SecurityContext.

files.name: JwtAuthenticationFilter.java
files.explanation: Runs before UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter; loads UserDetails from email claim.

codeExamples.title: Standardized API response envelope
codeExamples.description: ResponseWrapper factory methods keep controller responses consistent for frontends and OpenAPI examples.

files.name: ResponseWrapper.java
files.explanation: Static helpers: created(), found(), ok(), deleted(), badRequest(), notFound().

codeExamples.title: Paginated tourist place search
codeExamples.description: Controller builds Pageable from query DTO and delegates search to TouristPlaceService.

files.name: TouristPlaceController.java
files.explanation: Public GET /search — sort direction and field come from TouristPlaceSearchDTO.

codeExamples.title: Caffeine cache for country reads
codeExamples.description: CacheConfig registers named caches with 1-day expire-after-access for browse-heavy country endpoints.

files.name: CacheConfig.java
files.explanation: Four cache names for country query patterns; max 100 entries each.

codeExamples.title: Spring Security filter chain
codeExamples.description: Defines public auth/swagger routes, permissive GET access, and JWT filter placement.

files.name: SecurityConfig.java
files.explanation: CSRF disabled; stateless sessions; OAuth2 login for Google; review GET permitAll implications before prod.

features.title: JWT authentication & Google OAuth2
features.description: Email/password signup with activation tokens, JWT access/refresh pair, Redis token blacklist on logout, and optional Google social login via Spring OAuth2 Client.
highlights: Account activation and password reset via email tokens

features.title: Tourist place catalog & search
features.description: Paginated search, lookup by country/category, and CRUD for destinations with ratings, opening hours, and price range metadata.
highlights: Filter by country and place category
MapStruct mappers for entity ↔ DTO conversion

features.title: Country data with Caffeine cache
features.description: Country lookups cached in-process with Caffeine (1-day expire-after-access, max 100 entries per cache name).
highlights: Public GET endpoints for browse-heavy traffic

features.title: Reviews & average ratings
features.description: Users post reviews under /v1/api/user/reviews; system recalculates tourist place average ratings on create/update/delete.
highlights: Public read of reviews by place

features.title: User-curated place lists
features.description: Authenticated users create named lists and add/remove tourist places for trip planning.

features.title: Activities at destinations
features.description: Tours and experiences linked to tourist places with price, duration, and description.

features.title: Flyway migrations on PostgreSQL
features.description: Versioned SQL schema (V1__create_tables.sql) applied on startup; JPA ddl-auto validate ensures model alignment with RDS.

features.title: Redis token storage & blacklist
features.description: Activation and reset tokens plus JWT blacklist stored in Redis via StringRedisTemplate.
highlights: Logout invalidates access token in Redis
Use ElastiCache or Upstash on AWS

features.title: OpenAPI & standardized responses
features.description: springdoc-openapi exposes Swagger UI and a uniform ResponseWrapper envelope for success and error payloads.
highlights: Custom OpenAPI annotations per controller operation

features.title: Docker deploy for AWS EC2
features.description: Multi-stage Maven Docker image; docker-compose runs app container pointing to external RDS and Redis.
highlights: Local Postgres service commented out for cloud-first deploy

cloudServices.name: Amazon EC2
cloudServices.purpose: Runs Docker container (tourist_places_app) with Spring Boot API; security group allows 443 via ALB and optionally 8080 for direct access

cloudServices.name: Amazon RDS (PostgreSQL)
cloudServices.purpose: Primary database for countries, places, users, reviews, activities (DB_URL in .env)

cloudServices.name: Amazon ElastiCache Redis
cloudServices.purpose: Token blacklist, activation tokens, password reset tokens (SPRING_DATA_REDIS_HOST)

cloudServices.name: AWS Application Load Balancer
cloudServices.purpose: HTTPS termination with ACM certificate; forwards to EC2 target group on port 8080

cloudServices.name: Amazon SES
cloudServices.purpose: Transactional email for account activation and password reset (MAIL_HOST)

cloudServices.name: Google Cloud Console
cloudServices.purpose: OAuth2 client credentials for social login (GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET)

cloudServices.name: Amazon Route 53
cloudServices.purpose: DNS for api.yourdomain.com pointing to ALB

components.name: Travel frontend
components.description: SPA or mobile app consuming /v1/api/* and /auth/*

components.name: Content admin
components.description: ADMIN role tools for places, countries, activities

components.name: AWS ALB
components.description: TLS termination, health checks, optional path-based routing

components.name: EC2 security group
components.description: Allow 443 from ALB; restrict 8080 to VPC or disable public 8080

components.name: RDS PostgreSQL
components.description: Flyway migrations applied on app startup; SG allows only EC2/app subnet

components.name: ElastiCache Redis
components.description: Shared token store for multi-instance future scaling

components.name: RDS snapshots
components.description: Automated backups and point-in-time recovery enabled

components.name: Google OAuth2
components.description: Social login redirect URI must match production domain

components.name: ACM certificate
components.description: TLS cert attached to ALB for HTTPS

dockerFiles.description: Cloud-first compose: app only; PostgreSQL commented out—use RDS. Redis configured via SPRING_DATA_REDIS_* env vars.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  app:
    build: .
    container_name: tourist_places_app
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    env_file: .env
    environment:
      SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL: ${DB_URL}
      SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME: ${DB_USERNAME}
      SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
    networks:
      - app-network
# Cloud: Amazon RDS PostgreSQL + ElastiCache Redis

dockerFiles.description: Maven 3.9 + Temurin 17 build stage; JRE 17 runtime with app.jar.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-17 AS build
WORKDIR /app
COPY pom.xml .
RUN mvn dependency:go-offline -B
COPY src ./src
RUN mvn clean package -DskipTests -B

FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-jammy
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=build /app/target/*.jar app.jar
EXPOSE 8080
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

dockerFiles.description: Spring reads DB_URL, JWT_*, MAIL_*, and OAuth vars from .env via optional import.
